page: Google Cloud Platform m2-ultramem-208 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server powered by Intel Xeon processors. It features 208 dedicated virtual CPUs, derived from 104 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with an exceptionally large memory capacity of 5888.0 GB (5.75 TB), yielding approximately 28.31 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs.

Benchmark data reveals highly polarized performance characteristics. Due to its massive core count, the server achieves top-tier multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, alongside strong passmark CPU scores. However, single-core performance is weak across stress-ng, gzip compression, and Geekbench single-core tests. Memory bandwidth is a significant strength, with cached small-block read operations reaching top-tier speeds of 10,166.66 GB/sec, and large-block uncached reads and writes performing in the top 25%. Redis SET operations also rank in the top tier, executing 147.67 million operations per second.

This server represents an extreme resource-density profile designed for workloads that cannot be easily distributed across multiple smaller nodes. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high-density memory configuration carries a premium, making it inefficient for compute-bound tasks that do not utilize the massive RAM capacity. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs depending on the storage tier selected.

The m2-ultramem-208 is engineered specifically for large-scale in-memory databases, such as SAP HANA or Redis, massive data warehousing, enterprise resource planning systems, and heavy memory-bound data processing pipelines that require a single, massive shared-memory space.
description: Google Cloud Platform m2-ultramem-208 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 208 dedicated vCPUs and 5.75 TB of RAM. Powered by Intel Xeon processors running at 2.7 GHz, it contains no local storage or GPUs. Designed for massive memory-bound workloads, it delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and exceptional memory bandwidth, with cached small-block reads reaching 10,166.66 GB/sec and Redis SET operations exceeding 147 million operations per second. Conversely, single-core performance is weak, making it inefficient for single-threaded tasks. This high-density instance is qualitatively positioned as a premium solution for workloads requiring massive unified memory spaces rather than distributed architectures. It is best suited for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise ERP systems, and intensive data warehousing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform m2-ultramem-208 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 208 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 5.75 TB of RAM, and top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance for in-memory databases.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform m2-ultramem-208 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server with 208 dedicated vCPUs and 5.75 TB of RAM, built for large in-memory databases.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized platform featuring massive RAM capacity and top-tier multi-core performance for enterprise in-memory databases.
bullet_points: 208 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 5.75 TB of RAM
Top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core processing capabilities
Exceptional Redis performance with over 147 million operations per second
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Optimized for large-scale in-memory databases and enterprise ERPs
